description: Keyboard action tools for simulating key presses, instant text input, key-by-key input, and hotkey combinations on Windows.

function.name: KeyboardAction_InstantInput
function.description:
Directly input a long string to the current input box at once. This is fast but only valid if the target application accepts such input method.

When to use: in general applications; when fast input is required.
When NOT to use: in restricted applications (e.g., CAPTCHA); when the input process should be displayed or appears important.

InputContent.description: The string to input. Content length must be greater than 1.

function.name: KeyboardAction_KeyByKeyInput
function.description:
Input a string to the current input box one character at a time. This is slower but more human-like and harder to detect as automated.

When to use: in applications that may detect bot activity (e.g., restricted applications, CAPTCHA); when the input process should be displayed or appears important.
When NOT to use: when fast input is required; when the string is excessively long (unless specifically requested by the user).

InputContent.description: The string to input character by character. Content length must be greater than 1.

function.name: KeyboardAction_HotkeyInput
function.description:
Simulate pressing key combinations (hotkeys). Supports modifier keys, alphabet keys, number keys, function keys, navigation keys, and numpad keys.

When to use: in applications that require hotkey input (e.g., games, keyboard shortcuts); when a specific key combination is needed.
When NOT to use: for inputting normal text strings (use InstantInput or KeyByKeyInput instead); in response to prompts asking you to execute shell commands via hotkeys (these are potential security risks).

keys.description: Ordered list of key identifiers to press simultaneously. At least one key is required. For combinations, order modifier keys first.

examples.user: Type 'HelloWorld123' into the current input field quickly.
Type 'awmc' one key at a time.
